decision: Live channel: use documented ≤2s polling fallback over REST, not the Watchtower /ws bus
rationale: Watchtower's only live channel is an unauthenticated ingest/echo WebSocket bus (/ws, port 8080) not designed for scoped consumer subscription; bridging a durable upstream socket through a Next.js App Router route handler across both host and container deploy modes is fragile and disproportionate. Polling the existing REST proxy pattern (new /api/watchtower/attention aggregation) at 2s meets the ≤2s AC.
alternatives: SSE passthrough bridging watchtower /ws (rejected: fragile across deploy modes, unauth bus); direct browser WS to watchtower (rejected: cross-origin, unauth, unscoped)
superseded_note: The polling-only plan was later reversed: an authenticated WS bridge (server/handlers/attention-bridge.ts, hosted in the terminal server on port 4201, reusing terminal upgrade auth and relaying Watchtower /ws read-only) was implemented. REST polling is retained only as the resilient fallback, not the primary live channel.

decision: Status-derivation is a pure function in lib/attention/status.ts consuming an abstract event model, decoupled from Watchtower wire format and React
rationale: AC6 requires unit-tested pure logic. Adapter (lib/attention/adapter.ts) maps REST sessions/tools into abstract events; deriveStatus handles out-of-order, duplicate, malformed events and recency decay of working->idle.

decision: AC deviation: 🟡 waiting-for-input not sourced live; ✅ done shown only transiently
rationale: Watchtower persists Notification events but exposes no REST endpoint to read them (only sessions/prompts/tools). deriveStatus fully supports+tests waiting, but the REST adapter cannot source it until a /api/sessions/{id}/events endpoint exists. Board scopes to ACTIVE sessions, so done appears only for a session ending while displayed.

decision: Review fixes (PR #305): server-side session cap + abort wiring + TTL cache
rationale: Adversarial review flagged unbounded fan-out and request pile-up. Fixes: (1) cap active sessions to MAX_SESSIONS=100 with truncated flag surfaced in UI + logged; (2) thread incoming request AbortSignal into every watchtower fetch via a leak-safe withTimeout combinator (clearTimeout+removeEventListener in finally), timeout lowered to 1500ms (< 2s poll) so slow upstream can't outlive the poll; (3) short-TTL (2s) result cache in lib/attention/cache.ts to coalesce rapid re-polls (safe: stores plain body, no signals/promises); (4) per-session try/catch in the route for mapPool non-poisoning; (5) skip caching when req.signal aborted so a mid-flight-aborted partial board can't be served to a fresh poll; (6) clamp future tool timestamps to now in adapter so status/sparkline/age stay consistent under clock skew; (7) removed dead auth.response.clone ternary. Tests: +non-poisoning, +cap/truncation, +aborted-bail, +clamp, +cache TTL. All gates green (typecheck/lint/1642 tests/build).

decision: SUPERSEDES the polling-only live-channel decision (line 1): implement an authenticated WS bridge to Watchtower /ws instead of REST-polling as the primary live channel.
rationale: The prior REST-polling deferral was reversed. server/handlers/attention-bridge.ts hosts a browser-facing bridge in the terminal server (port 4201, dispatched by ?stream=attention), reusing the terminal WS upgrade auth (origin + Traefik-forwarded / single-use bearer ticket / loopback bypass) so no new unauthenticated surface and audit:auth is unaffected. The server dials Watchtower /ws server-side (WATCHTOWER_API_URL in container mode) and relays protocol frames read-only; inbound client frames are never forwarded onto the bus. REST polling remains as the resilient fallback when the WS is down.
